mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving immediate equity risk premium compression and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0 percent within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and industrial production signals synchronized demand-supply recovery, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising household credit balances indicate increasing consumer leverage, which historically fuels equity rallies during early-cycle expansions but triggers mean reversion and negative returns during late-cycle credit booms over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 70% of high-RSAFS growth periods, or the predictive power of credit growth flips sign across business cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly supports near-term corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO month-over-month changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5 percent, or the cross-sectional correlation between rate levels and forward returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls signal accelerating labor demand, which boosts aggregate consumption and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a period of elevated consumer credit indicate a construction-driven demand surge that typically overheats supply chains, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of high HOUST and RSAFS co-movement periods, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during recessionary episodes.
